About: It is about the Dark Lord Sauron's campaign to conquer all of Middle-earth.
It is about the quest to destroy the One Ring.
Affects: It affects the modern fantasy genre.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Middle-earth.
It is associated with the One Ring.
It is associated with the Dark Lord Sauron.
It is associated with Frodo.
It is associated with Sam.
It is associated with Merry.
It is associated with Pippin.
It is associated with Gandalf.
It is associated with Aragorn.
It is associated with Boromir.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the fantasy genre.
ConsistsOf: It consists of several appendices of chronologies, genealogies, and linguistic information.
CreatedBy: It was created by J. R. R. Tolkien.
HasA: It has numerous references in popular culture.
It has inspired many derivative works.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by extensive analysis of its themes, literary devices, and origins.
It is characterized by enduring popularity.
Influenced: It influenced the creation and shaping of the modern fantasy genre.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by earlier fantasy works.
It was influenced by Tolkien's own experiences in the First World War.
IsA: It is an epic high fantasy novel.
It is one of the best-selling books ever written.
It is considered one of the most influential fantasy books ever written.
It is Britain's best-loved novel of all time.
KnownAs: It is often called a trilogy.
KnownFor: It is known for its sequel to The Hobbit.
It is known for its main antagonist, the Dark Lord Sauron.
It is known for the One Ring.
It is known for its characters Frodo, Sam, Merry, and Pippin.
It is known for its characters Gandalf, Aragorn, Boromir, Legolas, and Gimli.
It is known for its themes.
It is known for its literary devices.
It is known for its origins.
LocatedIn: It is located in Middle-earth.
It is located in the Shire.
It is located in Rivendell.
It is located in the Mines of Moria.
It is located in Lothlórien.
It is located in Mordor.
It is located in the Emyn Muil.
It is located in Ithilien.
It is located in Minas Tirith.
It is located in Helm's Deep.
OccurredIn: It occurred in stages between 1937 and 1949.
It occurred in the Second Age.
It occurred in the Shire.
It occurred in Bree.
It occurred in Rivendell.
It occurred in the Mines of Moria.
It occurred in Lothlórien.
It occurred in Fangorn Forest.
It occurred in Edoras.
It occurred in Helm's Deep.
OriginatedIn: It originated in a sequel to The Hobbit.
PartOf: It is part of a two-volume set with The Silmarillion.
PublishedAs: It was published as a single volume in 1968.
It was published as a boxed set in 1957.
PublishedIn: It was published in three volumes.
It was published in stages from 29 July 1954 to 20 October 1955.
UsedIn: It is used in popular culture.
It is used in video games.
It is used in board games.
UsedTo: It is used to rally the Free Peoples of Middle-earth against Sauron's armies.
It is used to give Frodo a chance to destroy the One Ring.
WrittenBy: It was written by J. R. R. Tolkien.
WrittenIn: It was written in stages between 1937 and 1949.
